barRaise.topFinding: Twenty-eighth review, ninth straight healthy. First code-board movement in eight reviews: v0.52.0 landed (1260bbf, 06-22 14:06), a doc/version-bump release that committed the long-carried mobile-hooks comment repoints and synced every anchor to 0.52.0. The cycle's real event is the 06-23 canonical audit: it caught a CRITICAL working-tree FUSE truncation across three canonical docs (CONTEXT.md, ROADMAP.md, BACKLOG.md) before any release could commit the corruption, and that truncation has since been recovered cleanly with zero loss. The detect-and-recover defense worked end to end. But the same event cashed the standing observability MEDIUM for the first time in eight reviews: the dashboard audit block still reads 0/0/0 clean from the 06-22 release-time refresh, so the 06-23 CRITICAL never reached the dashboard. Eight reviews of 'benign because every skipped audit is clean' ended the day a skipped audit was a CRITICAL. The truncation is fixed so no harm shipped, but the invisible-finding scenario this MEDIUM has warned about is no longer hypothetical. Decouple the dashboard audit refresh from release time before 1.0.

actions.title: Decouple the dashboard audit block from release time: have the daily canonical-audit writer parse the high/medium/low counts from the latest CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md TL;DR and advance audit.latestReportPath, so a between-release HIGH cannot sit invisible. On 06-23 this demonstrably masked a real CRITICAL (FUSE truncation audit). Highest-value AI-ownable chore on the board.
Post-1.0, split the worker/src/index.js 5,510-line monolith into modules. No pre-1.0 action.
